pattern: Bucketing strings by a canonical key (their sorted letters) groups anagrams together in one pass, since two words are anagrams exactly when they share that key.
hints: Two strings are anagrams precisely when their multisets of characters match.
A canonical key that is identical for anagrams (sorted letters, or a 26-letter count signature) lets a hash map collect them.
For each string compute its sorted-character key, append the string to that key's bucket in a dictionary, then return the dictionary's value lists.

edge_inputs.description: several mutual anagrams plus a singleton
single empty string is its own group
exact duplicates land in the same group and must be preserved
no two strings are anagrams so every group is a singleton
multiple empty strings all group together

stress.description: random short lowercase words over a small alphabet so many anagram collisions occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        ln = rng.randrange(1, 5)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(ln)))
    return [words]
stress.note: tiny alphabet and short words force frequent anagram grouping and duplicates
